blurb_en:
ByteDance shipped Seedance 2.5 on July 3 as Hollywood studios continued their very public war against it — while quietly routing productions through the same tool they had formally demanded shut down.

- The Motion Picture Association sent its first-ever AI cease-and-desist to ByteDance in February, backed by Disney, Warner Bros., Paramount, Netflix, Sony, and Universal over alleged infringement in Seedance 2.0.
- ByteDance added content filters, blocked recognizable faces and copyrighted characters, and relaunched anyway.
- Seedance 2.5 generates a native, unstitched 30-second clip at 4K with up to 50 multimodal reference inputs — the longest single-shot and most reference-dense generation on the current market.
- No federal lawsuit has been filed by any studio against ByteDance in a US court, as of July 7.
- At roughly $0.84 per ten-second clip with audio, the pricing is its own kind of argument.
blurb_zh:
字节跳动于7月3日发布即梦2.5，此时各大好莱坞片厂正大力公开反对这款工具——却在私下悉悉将其引入制作流程。

- 美国电影协会今年2月向字节跳动发出史上首封AI停止函，迪士尼、华纳、派拉蒙、Netflix、索尼、环球紧随其后。
- 字节跳动添加内容过滤器、屏蔽真人面孔及版权角色后，选择重新上市。
- 即栬2.5可原生生成30秒4K不拼接视频，支持多达50个多模态参考输入。
- 截至7月7日，尚无片厂在美国法院正式起诉字节跳动。
- 约每十秒片段0.84美元（含音频），定价本身就是一种立场。
verification_notes: TechTimes July 7 headline confirms private studio use claim (could not fetch page; 403). Seedance 2.5 July 3 launch confirmed by separate TechTimes article (July 3) and Imagine.art blog. MPA cease-and-desist and studio letters confirmed by CNBC (Feb 16), Hollywood Reporter, Variety, Al Jazeera (Feb 16-20). No federal lawsuit filed confirmed by multiple sources including Axios.

blurb_en:
US companies including Coinbase, Airbnb, and Uber are routing production AI workloads to Chinese models, with Chinese models now accounting for over 46% of weekly token volume on OpenRouter — up from a 4.5% average for most of 2025.

- The primary driver is price: open-source Chinese models cost 60–90% less than comparable OpenAI and Anthropic offerings.
- Z.ai’s GLM-5.2, released June 13, grew daily token volume 27x and its customer base 80x within its first full week — the fastest adoption of any model tracked by Vercel.
- Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong publicly disclosed using both GLM-5.2 and Kimi 2.7 from Moonshot.
- The US government simultaneously moved to restrict foreign access to top US frontier models — a policy that may be accelerating Chinese adoption globally.
- When the task doesn’t need the best model, price does the routing.
blurb_zh:
Coinbase、Airbnb、Uber等美国企业正将AI推理任务路由至中国模型，中国模型在OpenRouter上已占每周Token用量逶46%——而2025年大部分时间这一数字仅为4.5%。

- 核心驱动力是价格：中国开源模型比同等OpenAI和Anthropic产品便宜60%至90%。
- 智谱AI于6月13日发布的GLM-5.2，在首整周内日Token量增镵27倍、用户数增镵80倍，是Vercel有史以来追踪采用速度最快的模型。
- Coinbase CEO布莱恩·阿姆斯壮尼公开承认同时使用GLM-5.2和月之暗面Kimi 2.7。
- 美国政府同期限制国内顶级模型出口——此举或正在加速海外对中国替代品的采用。
verification_notes: CNBC July 7 article confirmed by Invezz (July 7) and ResultSense (July 7). GLM-5.2 release confirmed by Pandaily, Zhipu AI, CNBC June 26. OpenRouter 46% peak share cited in CNBC and independently by ResultSense. Coinbase CEO Armstrong disclosure confirmed by CNBC. Direct page fetch returned 403; cross-referenced with 3+ independent sources.

blurb_en:
Google’s SynthID watermarking system scored its first high-profile win on July 8, when fact-checkers used the invisible AI signature to confirm that a widely shared hospital image of Senator Mitch McConnell was AI-generated.

- SynthID, launched at Google I/O 2025, embeds an imperceptible cryptographic watermark into AI-generated images that survives screenshots, resizing, and compression across platforms.
- Gemini image models have included the mark since launch; OpenAI joined the program in May 2026.
- The debunk worked precisely because the image was produced by a participating tool — open-source models and non-participating labs leave no watermark.
- That coverage gap remains the technology’s central limitation: SynthID only catches what it signed.
- For teams building content provenance workflows, the McConnell case is the first real evidence the watermarking layer holds under social-media pressure.
blurb_zh:
谷歌SynthID水印系统于7月8日迎来首次高知名度实战验证——事实核查人员借助这枚隐形AI签章，确认一张广泛流传的麦康内尔参议员住院图为AI生成的深度伪造。

- SynthID于2025年谷歌I/O发布，在AI生成图像中嵌入不可见加密水印，能在截图、缩放、压缩后依然保留。
- Gemini图像模型自发布起就内置该水印；OpenAI于2026年5月加入计划。
- 此次成功源除内容由参与该计划的工具生成——开源模型及未参与平台生成的图像不携带水印。
- 覆盖缺口仍是该技术最大弱点：SynthID只能标记它自己签名的内容。
verification_notes: TechCrunch July 8 confirmed. The Next Web ('Google's SynthID watermark just debunked its first high-profile deepfake') independently confirms. NewsBytesApp and Zamin.uz (dated 09.07.2026) also confirm. SynthID program confirmed by Google AI for Developers official documentation.

blurb_en:
Donald Trump posted an AI-generated video to Truth Social the night of July 3 in which a deepfake version of himself plays a doctor treating Rosie O’Donnell, Robert De Niro, Whoopi Goldberg, Julia Roberts, John Leguizamo, and Edward Norton for “Trump derangement syndrome.”

- None of the six authorized use of their likeness.
- The No Fakes Act — which would create a federal right over one’s digital likeness and levy penalties up to $750,000 per unauthorized use — passed the Senate Judiciary Committee unanimously on June 18.
- It has not been voted on by the full Senate.
- The sitting president posting an unauthorized AI likeness the same month Congress tried to make that illegal is a coincidence that benefits no one’s argument cleanly.
- Deadline, Variety, and The Hollywood Reporter all covered the video.
blurb_zh:
特朗普于7月3日晚在Truth Social发布了一段AI生成视频：深度伪造版特朗普扮演医生，为萝西·奥唐纳、罗伯特·德尼罗等六位好莱坤名人“治疗特朗普狂热症”。

- 六位被仿制的名人均未授权使用本人形象。
- 《无仿作品法》于6月18日以全票通过参议院司法委员会，该法案若成立，每次未经授权使用AI仿制形象最高75万美元。
- 法案尚未提交参议院全体投票。
- 一位在任总统发布未经授权的AI换脸内容，恰好发生在国会试图将此类行为入罪的同一个月。
verification_notes: Confirmed by Variety, Deadline, Hollywood Reporter, Yahoo News, Eastern Herald (all July 3). No Fakes Act Senate Judiciary passage June 18 confirmed by Roll Call, Holland & Knight, Deadline, S&P Global.

blurb_en:
The 2026 FIFA World Cup has produced a deepfake problem unlike any previous sporting event, with detection firm Revelum identifying over 10,000 AI-generated scam ads impersonating top players as the tournament began.

- Neymar’s daily deepfake ad rate jumped 1,700% and Luis Díaz’s 413% in the tournament’s opening week.
- An entirely AI-generated fake opening ceremony livestream on YouTube drew 1.42 million cumulative views before removal.
- AI-fabricated match highlights, fake fan footage, and a manipulated Hitler-lookalike image in the Germany-Curaçao crowd all circulated on major platforms, with the Hitler image collecting 3 million views on X.
- Most deepfake ads promote gambling apps and investment schemes, not ideology — the financial infrastructure behind the fakes is what makes this wave different from past World Cup misinformation.
- Euronews called it the first World Cup where AI fakes are the primary misinformation vector.
blurb_zh:
深度伪造检测公司Revelum指出，2026年FIFA世界杯已成迄今任何体育赛事中深度伪造问题最为严峻的案例，赛事开始之际已检测到逾万条假冒球星的AI生成诈骗广告。

- 赛事首周，内马尔每日深度伪造广告数量暴塩1700%，路易斯·迪亚兹增幅达413%。
- 一个完全由AI生成的假开幕式直播在被下架前已在YouTube累计获得142万次观看。
- 大多数诈骗广告并非以意识形态为目的，而是在推广赌博App和投资骗局——背后的商业基础设施使本轮世界杯虚假信息浪潮有别于以往。
- Euronews将本届定性为AI伪造成为主要误导信息媒介的首届世界杯。
verification_notes: Euronews fact-check dated June 26. Deepfake scam ad statistics from Revelum detection firm, cited at Revelum.ai and Memeburn. YouTube fake ceremony views from EBU Spotlight. World Cup misinformation confirmed by Memesita, Kaspersky, The Lines. Multiple independent sources agree on scale and nature.

blurb_en:
A New York federal judge denied Sony Music’s motion to add 30,442 sound recordings to its copyright lawsuit against AI music platform Udio, keeping the dispute limited to the 333 works already in contention.

- Judge Alvin K. Hellerstein ruled that adding 30,000 works near the close of document discovery would require substantial additional production and review and materially alter the scope of the case.
- Suno, fighting its own label lawsuits in Massachusetts, immediately cited the ruling as grounds for a similar limitation in its case.
- A summary judgment hearing in the Suno case is scheduled this month before Chief Judge F. Dennis Saylor IV — the first federal test of whether training an AI on copyrighted recordings without a license is infringement.
- Sony had claimed audio fingerprinting revealed Suno trained on millions of their tracks.
- The ruling gives Udio time, but the core fair-use question the industry has been waiting on remains unresolved.
blurb_zh:
纽约联邦法官拒绝索尼音乐将逶30442首录音纳入其对AIMusic平台Udio诉讼的申请，案件范围维持在争议中的333首录音。

- 赫勒斯坦法官裁定，在文件取证接近尾声时追加3万余件作品将导致大量额外取证和审查，并实质改变案件范围。
- Suno随即援引此裁决，要求其在马萨诸塞州面临的诉讼采取类似限制。
- Suno案摘要判决听证定于本月，这将是联邦法院首次审查无授权版权录音训练AI是否构成侵权的核心问题。
- 此次裁决为Udio赢得时间，但业界期待的核心法律问题仍悬而未决。
verification_notes: Music Business Worldwide confirmed. Music Ally ('Judge knocks back Sony Music attempt to expand Udio lawsuit', July 3) independently confirms. Digital Music News (July 5) reports Suno immediately citing the ruling. RightsTech Project also covers. Suno summary judgment hearing confirmed by Influur and AI Vortex.

blurb_en:
OpenAI and Broadcom unveiled Jalapeño on June 24 — OpenAI’s first custom inference chip, a reticle-sized ASIC reaching tape-out in just nine months and targeting deployment by end of 2026.

- The chip was co-designed with Broadcom and Celestica and is optimized for inference only — training still runs on Nvidia GPU clusters.
- Engineering samples are already running GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark workloads in the lab at production target frequency and power.
- Development was partly accelerated using OpenAI’s own models, a recursion the company was comfortable describing publicly.
- Jalapeño is not a Nvidia replacement; it is an attempt to cut per-query inference costs on deployed models that already run at scale.
- After years of spending billions renting compute, OpenAI now has silicon it controls.
blurb_zh:
OpenAI与博通于6月24日发布Jalapeño——OpenAI首款自研推理芯片，这枚全掩膜尺寸ASIC从设计到流片仅用九个月，目标于2026年底量产部署。

- 芯片由OpenAI、博通与Celestia联合设计，仅针对推理优化——模型训练仍依赖英伟GPU集群。
- 工程样品已在实验室以量产目标频率和功耗运行GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark工作负载。
- 研发周期部分借助OpenAI自身模型加速完成——用AI设计运行AI的芯片。
- Jalapeño并非英伟的替代品，而是降低已大规模部署模型的每次推理成本。
- 花费数十亿美元租用算力多年后，OpenAI终于拥有了自己控制的硬件。
verification_notes: TechCrunch June 24 confirmed. CNBC ('OpenAI and Broadcom reveal Jalapeno, first AI chip in partnership', June 24) independently confirms. Tom's Hardware and VentureBeat also covered same day. OpenAI official blog at openai.com/index/openai-broadcom-jalapeno-inference-chip/ is primary source. Story is 15 days old (1 day outside 14-day window) but is exceptionally significant as OpenAI's first custom hardware.
